steps.title: Identify what would weaken the claim
steps.content: The student claims joining an FTA increases the rate of agricultural export growth. Data that weaken this would show a country whose growth rate fell (or stayed flat) after joining an FTA.

steps.title: Match the choice
steps.content:
"Although agricultural exports from Morocco grew over the five years after Morocco joined MAFTA, their growth rate was even higher in the five years before MAFTA.": Morocco's growth rate after joining MAFTA was lower than its rate before MAFTA — directly contradicting the student's claim.

A is consistent with the claim. B and C show post-FTA improvements that support, not weaken, the claim.

That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
